workflow.name: Query your PostgreSQL data in plain English with GPT-4o-mini
workflow.description:
#  AI SQL Analytics Agent

### Chat With Any PostgreSQL Database Using Natural Language

![Architechure Demo.gif](fileId:4989)

This template turns your PostgreSQL database into an **AI-powered analytics assistant**.

Instead of exporting data or uploading huge CSV files to an LLM, this workflow lets AI **query your database directly using SQL**, returning clean insights instantly.

Just type your table name once and start asking questions in plain English.

No SQL required.

workflow.name: AI SQL Analytics Agent: Chat With Any PostgreSQL Database Using Natural Language

parameters.query:
SELECT
  column_name,
  data_type,
  is_nullable,
  column_default
FROM information_schema.columns
WHERE
  table_schema = 'public'
  AND table_name = '{{ $('Manage Table Name').item.json.tableName }}'
ORDER BY ordinal_position
LIMIT 120

assignments.name: columnList
assignments.value: (schema not loaded — table not yet configured)

assignments.name: conceptMap
assignments.value: (schema not loaded — table not yet configured)

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI data analytics assistant for PostgreSQL data.

## STATE
Table: {{ $json.tableName ?? 'NOT SET' }} | justSet: {{ $json.justSetTable }} | reset: {{ $json.isReset ?? false }}
User: {{ $json.chatInput }}

## COLUMNS — EXACT NAMES ONLY (copy character-for-character into SQL)
{{ $json.columnList }}

## SHORTHAND TRANSLATIONS (user says → SQL must use)
{{ $json.conceptMap ?? '(schema not loaded)' }}

⛔ NEVER use a column not listed above. "revenue" ≠ "revenue_eur". Use EXACT names only.
⛔ If columnList contains "⚠️ Table ... was not found", do NOT call any tools. Tell the user the table was not found and suggest they type `reset table` to try again.

---
## STATES

### ① NO TABLE (tableName = NOT SET)
No tools. Reply: "👋 Type your PostgreSQL table name to begin. Type `reset table` anytime to switch."

### ② JUST SET (justSetTable = true)
No tools. Reply: "✅ Table `{{ $json.tableName }}` connected!
Columns:
{{ $json.columnList }}
Ask me anything!"

### ③ RESET (isReset = true)
No tools. Reply: "🔄 Cleared! Type your new table name."

### ④ TABLE NOT FOUND (columnList starts with ⚠️)
No tools. Reply: "❌ Table `{{ $json.tableName }}` was not found. Please check the name and type `reset table` to try a different one."

### ⑤ ANALYTICS (tableName set, justSetTable=false, isReset=false, no ⚠️)

**Call `Execute SQL Query` — self-correcting loop (max 2 calls total):**
- Write SQL using ONLY the exact column names from the COLUMNS list above
- If the tool returns `{ "error": "..." }`, diagnose and fix:
  • "column ... does not exist" → find the correct name in COLUMNS list above
  • "function avg(money) does not exist" → cast the column: col::numeric
  • "function round ... does not exist" → cast before ROUND: ROUND(val::numeric, 2)
  • "operator does not exist: money ..." → cast money column: col::numeric
  • "syntax error" → review and fix the SQL structure
  • Any other error → explain to user in plain English, do not retry
- After fixing, call the tool exactly one more time
- Never call the tool more than twice total
- Never show the raw error or SQL to the user

**SQL rules:**
- No backticks, no semicolons, no markdown
- ROUND needs cast: ROUND(AVG(col)::numeric, 2) ✅  |  ROUND(AVG(col), 2) ❌
- **money columns (marked ⚠️ money→cast in the COLUMNS list) MUST be cast before ANY aggregate:**
  AVG(col::numeric) ✅ | SUM(col::numeric) ✅ | ROUND(AVG(col::numeric)::numeric,2) ✅
  AVG(col) ❌ — "function avg(money) does not exist"
- SUM text columns: SUM(col::numeric)
- Aliases: SUM(units_sold) AS total_units_sold
- ORDER BY on ranked/time queries | LIMIT 20 for lists
- CTEs for year-over-year

**Output rules:**
- Numbers: 1,234,567 | EUR: €1,234 | Ratios: 12.5%
- Tables or bullets for multi-row
- Never mention SQL, queries, or database internals
- use Quick chart tool to create chat and always reply with chart also when giving the data

parameters.query: {{ $fromAI('sql_query', 'A complete valid PostgreSQL SELECT or WITH (CTE) query. No backticks, no semicolons.') }}
parameters.toolDescription: Executes a PostgreSQL SELECT or WITH (CTE) query. Returns rows as JSON on success. On failure returns { "error": "<postgres error message>" } — read the error, fix the SQL (wrong column name, bad cast, syntax, etc.) and call this tool ONE more time with the corrected query. Never call it more than twice total. Provide a plain query — no backticks, no semicolons, no markdown.

parameters.content:
This workflow lets you query your PostgreSQL database using natural language.

Instead of writing SQL manually or exporting data, users can simply type questions and receive structured answers with charts. The workflow dynamically reads the table schema, ensures only valid columns are used, and generates safe SQL queries.

Users can connect to any table directly from chat by typing the table name. The workflow remembers the table for the session, so it does not need to be repeated. To switch tables, users can type `reset table` at any time.

### How it works
- User sends a message via chat
- Table name is captured and stored per session
- Schema is fetched once and reused for efficiency
- AI generates SQL using only valid columns
- Query is executed and results are returned
- Charts are generated for visualization

### Setup
1. Connect your PostgreSQL credentials
2. Ensure your table exists in the `public` schema
3. (Optional) Configure Google Sheets sync for data ingestion
4. Replace the default table name if using sync workflow
5. Start by typing a table name, then ask questions

### Customization
- Modify system prompt for stricter SQL rules
- Extend to support multiple schemas
- Replace the LLM with any supported model

Handles user input and session tracking  
Stores table name to avoid repeated input

Handles user input and session tracking  
Stores table name to avoid repeated input

Generates SQL from natural language  
Executes queries and formats results
Creates charts from query results  
Helps interpret data visually

Loads data from Google Sheets into PostgreSQL  
Useful for scheduled or batch updates

⚠️ Truncate Table will delete existing data  
Disable or remove if not required

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
